question: It's easy to __________ a conversation if you start by commenting on your surroundings.
Read: 'It turned out that the girl had a rare disease, but her sight was saved!' — The phrase 'it turned out' means:
She __________ already __________ her project — she can relax now.
Good teachers know how to __________ complex ideas in a simple way.
She has been working hard all day, __________?
I am not hungry at all. I have __________ eaten — just twenty minutes ago.
It was cloudy, but she could still __________ the outline of the mountains.
The student __________ her essay last night before the midnight deadline.
What is the purpose of the CONCLUSION in an analytical essay?
choices: To summarise what has been argued
To ask the reader a question
To list all the sources used

question: I __________ never __________ anyone as enthusiastic as she is about learning.

Read: 'Jordan is in a good position for using AI as it is at the forefront of technology. The people looking into using AI in Jordan are hoping it will help in science, investment and government services.' — Name TWO areas where AI is hoped to help Jordan.

Read: 'Jane pointed out that the animals couldn't communicate using language like people, but they could communicate by touch and sound. Amazingly, she also realised they could use sign language.' — How do chimpanzees communicate?
choices: By touch, sound and sign language

question: She kindly __________ that my presentation had a small factual mistake.
She __________ to her grandmother every Sunday without fail — it's a family tradition.
She __________ never __________ to the cinema on weekdays.
Which phrase best expresses SURPRISE or DISBELIEF?
choices: I can breathe a sigh of relief now.
I'm feeling a bit down in the dumps.
You've got to be kidding me!

question: She __________ all morning on her project and her eyes are very tired.
The situation __________ better slowly — things are improving week by week.
He __________ always __________ his keys — it really gets on everyone's nerves!
She felt very __________ as she sat outside the exam room waiting to go in.
He is still talking about that argument from last year — he simply __________.
choices: can't get a word in edgeways

question: I __________ to Jordan three times and I love it more each time.
Students need time to __________ new grammar rules before practising them.
He __________ at least ten texts every day for the past month.
Read: 'French, Spanish and German are three of the languages identified as the most important.' — Which THREE languages are identified as the most important in the UK?
I __________ working here in recent years and I really enjoy my job.
He __________ thank-you letters all morning — his hand is very tired now.
A: 'My parents are taking me to Italy!' B: 'I don't __________ you! You lucky thing!'
My teacher is very good at __________ difficult ideas in a simple way.
'That's a weight off my __________.' — Complete the expression of relief.
He promised to __________ the message to his team first thing in the morning.
Read: 'I feel that we may well learn even more about animal communication in the future.' — This sentence expresses the author's:
choices: Hope or prediction about the future

question: Suha was bewildered by the maths questions in the exam, __________?
I haven't spoken to her __________ she moved to Aqaba last year.
I'm still your best friend after all this, __________?
The coach was __________ when he saw the referee's unfair decision.

Read: 'People are also using AI more and more in everyday life and although it might make many aspects of life easier, it could also make things worse, especially for our children.' — This sentence presents:
choices: Only the positive side of AI
Only the negative side of AI
Both a positive and a negative side of AI

question: When I __________ to school, it was raining hard outside.
They __________ immediately and have been inseparable ever since.
Look! The kitchen tap __________ — we need to call a plumber right away.
